author: ghyls
body:
#### PR description:


```
----- Begin Fatal Exception 04-May-2026 17:27:46 CEST-----------------------
An exception of category 'MPI' occurred while
   [0] Calling InputSource::readEvent_
Exception Message:
MPIChannel::wait() cannot resolve a kBusy status
----- End Fatal Exception -------------------------------------------------
```


The probability of this happening seems to be larger the larger is the number of
MPI operations that are active at a given time.


I *think* this is the sequence that triggers it:
- An event has just been processed. The controller has calls
  `MPIChannel::sync()`, posts its `MPI_Ibarrier`, and set its `status_` to
  `kSync`.
- On the other side, the source also calls `MPIChannel::sync()` at the end of
  the event. Now in super slow motion:
   - [1] The source, in `MPIChannel::sync()`, calls `MPI_Ibarrier`.
- In at the *same* time, the controller (which has already started with the next
  event) enters `MPIChannel::ready()`
   - Reads `status_ = kSync`.
   - `MPI_Test` passes (sice both barriers have been called), `flag` is set to 1.
   - This slot is selected for the next event.
   - Controller calls `follower.sendEvent(event.eventAuxiliary(), this slot);`
- Another source thread, running `setRunAndEventInfo`, receives this messaage.
   - `MPISource::produce` is called
      - `channel_->wait()` is called
         - status is still `kBusy`, which triggers the error

If instead of triggering the error we `yield()`, this worker thread could be
re-assigned to the thread that just ran [1], so it can move two lines forward
and set its status to `kSync`. 

#### PR validation:

None

#### Backports

This PR will be backported to `16_0_X` and to `16_1_X`
title: Prevent a race condition between MPI threads from `throw`ing an error
